page: Microsoft Azure E16_v4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Ev4 server family. It is built on the x86_64 architecture and powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8272CL processor running at 2.6 GHz. The configuration features 16 vCPUs derived from 8 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, resulting in 2.0 threads per core. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. The processor includes a 35 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and AVX-512_VNNI.

This instance type does not include any local NVMe or SSD storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via external network-attached volumes. There are no bundled GPUs, making it unsuitable for hardware-accelerated graphics or heavy parallel computing workloads. Additionally, it does not provide any complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark results position the E16_v4 consistently in the average performance tier across multiple categories. It achieves a stress-ng single-core score of 1411.28 and a multi-core score of 14807.48. Memory performance is characterized by a read-only small block cached bandwidth of 831.3 GB/sec and an uncached large block write bandwidth of 45.84 GB/sec. In application-specific tests, it delivers 14.0 million operations per second on Redis SET operations and a static web serving throughput of 12.13 GB/sec. For machine learning, prompt processing with a small 135M LLM model reaches 696.1 tokens per second, though performance drops to a weak rating of 22.92 tokens per second for a medium 7B model.

Given its high memory density relative to compute resources, the E16_v4 offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads that do not require local high-speed storage or dedicated GPU acceleration. It is well-suited for relational databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, enterprise applications, and moderate data processing tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure E16_v4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the Ev4 family, featuring 16 vCPUs and 128.0 GB of RAM. Powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8272CL processor running at 2.6 GHz, this x86_64 server provides 8.0 GB of memory per core. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark data places its performance in the average tier, with a multi-core stress-ng score of 14807.48 and a Redis SET throughput of 14.0 million operations per second. The high memory-to-vCPU ratio makes it qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-intensive workloads such as databases, in-memory caching, and enterprise applications that do not require local NVMe storage.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E16_v4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 16 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor. It is designed for memory-intensive workloads without local storage requirements.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E16_v4 is an Ev4-family virtual machine with 16 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, optimized for memory-heavy workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio and dedicated Intel Xeon processing for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
